data.content:
# Clawdbot

Clawdbot is an AI-powered personal assistant that connects to multiple messaging platforms including WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Slack, Signal, iMessage, and Microsoft Teams. It runs as a Gateway server that manages WebSocket connections, agent sessions, and message routing across all configured channels, providing a unified conversational AI experience across different communication platforms.

The core architecture consists of a Gateway server that owns channel connections (via libraries like Baileys for WhatsApp, grammY for Telegram, and Discord.js for Discord), an embedded agent runtime for processing messages, and a CLI for configuration and management. Clawdbot supports multi-agent setups, workspace-based skills, browser automation, web search/fetch tools, and persistent session management with automatic context compaction.

## CLI Installation

Install Clawdbot globally via npm or the install script.

```bash
# Quick install via script
curl -fsSL https://clawd.bot/install.sh | bash

# Or via npm
npm install -g clawdbot@latest

# Or via pnpm
pnpm add -g clawdbot@latest

# Windows (PowerShell)
iwr -useb https://clawd.bot/install.ps1 | iex
```

## Onboarding Wizard

Run the interactive setup wizard to configure authentication, channels, and the background daemon.

```bash
# Full onboarding with daemon installation
clawdbot onboard --install-daemon

# The wizard configures:
# - Model/auth (OAuth or API keys)
# - Gateway settings
# - Channel providers (WhatsApp QR, Telegram/Discord tokens)
# - Pairing defaults for secure DMs
# - Workspace bootstrap + skills
```

## Gateway Server

The Gateway is Clawdbot's WebSocket server managing channels, nodes, sessions, and hooks.

```bash
# Start the gateway (foreground)
clawdbot gateway --port 18789 --verbose

# Start with specific bind mode
clawdbot gateway --bind lan --auth token --token "my-secret-token"

# Check gateway health
clawdbot gateway health --url ws://127.0.0.1:18789

# Query gateway status (probes local + remote)
clawdbot gateway status
clawdbot gateway status --json

# Remote gateway over SSH
clawdbot gateway status --ssh user@gateway-host

# Low-level RPC call
clawdbot gateway call status
clawdbot gateway call logs.tail --params '{"sinceMs": 60000}'

# Discover gateways via Bonjour
clawdbot gateway discover --timeout 4000
clawdbot gateway discover --json | jq '.beacons[].wsUrl'
```

## WhatsApp Channel Setup

Configure WhatsApp Web integration using Baileys for QR-based linking.

```bash
# Login via QR code (scan with WhatsApp -> Linked Devices)
clawdbot channels login

# Multi-account login
clawdbot channels login --account work

# Check channel status
clawdbot channels status

# Logout (deletes auth state)
clawdbot channels logout
```

```json5
// ~/.clawdbot/clawdbot.json - WhatsApp configuration
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      dmPolicy: "pairing",           // pairing | allowlist | open | disabled
      allowFrom: ["+15551234567"],   // E.164 phone numbers
      selfChatMode: false,           // true if using personal number
      sendReadReceipts: true,
      groupPolicy: "allowlist",
      groups: { "*": { requireMention: true } },
      textChunkLimit: 4000,
      mediaMaxMb: 50,
      ackReaction: {
        emoji: "eyes",
        direct: true,
        group: "mentions"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Telegram Bot Setup

Configure Telegram bot integration using the Bot API with grammY.

```bash
# Set bot token via environment
export TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN="123:abc"

# Or create via BotFather:
# 1. Chat with @BotFather
# 2. Run /newbot and follow prompts
# 3. Copy token to config
```

```json5
// ~/.clawdbot/clawdbot.json - Telegram configuration
{
  channels: {
    telegram: {
      enabled: true,
      botToken: "123:abc",
      dmPolicy: "pairing",
      allowFrom: ["123456789"],      // Telegram user IDs
      groupPolicy: "allowlist",
      groupAllowFrom: ["987654321"],
      groups: {
        "*": { requireMention: true },
        "-1001234567890": { requireMention: false }
      },
      capabilities: {
        inlineButtons: "allowlist"   // off | dm | group | all | allowlist
      },
      textChunkLimit: 4000,
      streamMode: "partial",         // off | partial | block
      customCommands: [
        { command: "backup", description: "Git backup" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

## Discord Bot Setup

Configure Discord bot integration for DMs and guild channels.

```bash
# Set bot token via environment
export DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN="YOUR_BOT_TOKEN"

# Required Discord Developer Portal setup:
# 1. Create Application -> Bot -> Add Bot
# 2. Enable Message Content Intent + Server Members Intent
# 3. OAuth2 URL Generator: scopes=bot,applications.commands
# 4. Invite bot with View/Send/Read History permissions
```

```json5
// ~/.clawdbot/clawdbot.json - Discord configuration
{
  channels: {
    discord: {
      enabled: true,
      token: "YOUR_BOT_TOKEN",
      dm: {
        enabled: true,
        policy: "pairing",           // pairing | allowlist | open | disabled
        allowFrom: ["123456789012345678"]
      },
      groupPolicy: "allowlist",
      guilds: {
        "*": { requireMention: true },
        "123456789012345678": {
          requireMention: false,
          users: ["987654321098765432"],
          channels: {
            help: {
              allow: true,
              requireMention: true,
              skills: ["search", "docs"]
            }
          }
        }
      },
      historyLimit: 20,
      textChunkLimit: 2000,
      actions: {
        reactions: true,
        polls: true,
        threads: true,
        moderation: false
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Message Sending CLI

Send messages and perform channel actions across all supported platforms.

```bash
# Send a WhatsApp message
clawdbot message send --channel whatsapp --target +15555550123 --message "Hello!"

# Send with media
clawdbot message send --channel whatsapp --target +15555550123 \
  --message "Check this out" --media /path/to/image.jpg

# Send a Discord reply
clawdbot message send --channel discord \
  --target channel:123456789 --message "hi" --reply-to 987654321

# Create a Discord poll
clawdbot message poll --channel discord \
  --target channel:123456789 \
  --poll-question "Snack?" \
  --poll-option Pizza --poll-option Sushi \
  --poll-multi --poll-duration-hours 48

# React to a message (Discord/Slack/Telegram/WhatsApp)
clawdbot message react --channel slack \
  --target C123 --message-id 456 --emoji "white_check_mark"

# Send Telegram inline buttons
clawdbot message send --channel telegram --target @mychat --message "Choose:" \
  --buttons '[[{"text":"Yes","callback_data":"cmd:yes"}],[{"text":"No","callback_data":"cmd:no"}]]'

# Broadcast to multiple targets
clawdbot message broadcast --channel all \
  --targets "+15555550123" --targets "@telegram_user" \
  --message "Important announcement"

# Thread operations (Discord)
clawdbot message thread create --channel discord \
  --thread-name "Discussion" --target channel:123

clawdbot message thread reply --channel discord \
  --target thread:456 --message "Thread reply"
```

## Agent Direct Runs

Run agent turns directly without inbound chat messages.

```bash
# Run agent turn via Gateway
clawdbot agent --to +15555550123 --message "status update"

# With specific session
clawdbot agent --session-id 1234 --message "Summarize inbox" --thinking medium

# With delivery back to channel
clawdbot agent --to +15555550123 --message "Generate report" --deliver

# Local run (requires API keys in shell)
clawdbot agent --local --to +15555550123 --message "Quick task"

# JSON output with verbose logging
clawdbot agent --to +15555550123 --message "Debug info" --verbose on --json
```

## Pairing and Access Control

Manage secure DM pairing and access control for unknown senders.

```bash
# List pending pairing requests
clawdbot pairing list whatsapp
clawdbot pairing list telegram
clawdbot pairing list discord

# Approve a pairing code (codes expire after 1 hour)
clawdbot pairing approve whatsapp ABC123
clawdbot pairing approve telegram XYZ789

# Check overall status
clawdbot status
clawdbot status --all    # Full debug report
clawdbot health          # Gateway health snapshot
```

## Browser Automation

Control a dedicated Chrome/Brave/Edge browser profile for agent automation.

```bash
# Browser lifecycle
clawdbot browser --browser-profile clawd status
clawdbot browser --browser-profile clawd start
clawdbot browser --browser-profile clawd stop

# Tab management
clawdbot browser tabs
clawdbot browser open https://example.com
clawdbot browser tab new
clawdbot browser tab select 2
clawdbot browser tab close 2

# Page inspection
clawdbot browser snapshot                           # AI snapshot with refs
clawdbot browser snapshot --interactive --compact   # Role snapshot
clawdbot browser snapshot --labels                  # Screenshot with overlays
clawdbot browser screenshot
clawdbot browser screenshot --full-page
clawdbot browser pdf

# Actions (use ref from snapshot)
clawdbot browser click 12
clawdbot browser type 23 "hello" --submit
clawdbot browser hover 44
clawdbot browser select 9 OptionA OptionB
clawdbot browser navigate https://example.com

# Wait conditions
clawdbot browser wait "#main" --url "**/dash" --load networkidle

# State management
clawdbot browser cookies
clawdbot browser cookies set session abc123 --url "https://example.com"
clawdbot browser set offline on
clawdbot browser set device "iPhone 14"
```

```json5
// ~/.clawdbot/clawdbot.json - Browser configuration
{
  browser: {
    enabled: true,
    defaultProfile: "clawd",
    controlUrl: "http://127.0.0.1:18791",
    headless: false,
    executablePath: "/Applications/Brave Browser.app/Contents/MacOS/Brave Browser",
    profiles: {
      clawd: { cdpPort: 18800, color: "#FF4500" },
      work: { cdpPort: 18801, color: "#0066CC" },
      browserless: {
        cdpUrl: "https://production-sfo.browserless.io?token=<API_KEY>",
        color: "#00AA00"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Web Search and Fetch

Search the web and fetch content using Brave Search API.

```bash
# Configure Brave API key
clawdbot configure --section web
```

```json5
// ~/.clawdbot/clawdbot.json - Web tools configuration
{
  tools: {
    web: {
      search: {
        enabled: true,
        apiKey: "BRAVE_API_KEY_HERE",
        maxResults: 5,
        timeoutSeconds: 30,
        cacheTtlMinutes: 15
      },
      fetch: {
        enabled: true,
        maxChars: 50000,
        timeoutSeconds: 30,
        readability: true,
        firecrawl: {
          enabled: true,
          apiKey: "FIRECRAWL_API_KEY_HERE"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

```javascript
// Agent tool usage examples
// Web search with regional targeting
await web_search({
  query: "AI news",
  count: 10,
  country: "US",
  search_lang: "en"
});

// Fetch and extract page content
await web_fetch({
  url: "https://example.com/article",
  extractMode: "markdown",
  maxChars: 10000
});
```

## Skills System

Manage and configure AgentSkills-compatible skill folders.

```bash
# Install skills from ClawdHub
clawdhub install <skill-slug>

# Update all installed skills
clawdhub update --all

# Sync and publish skill updates
clawdhub sync --all
```

```json5
// ~/.clawdbot/clawdbot.json - Skills configuration
{
  skills: {
    entries: {
      "nano-banana-pro": {
        enabled: true,
        apiKey: "GEMINI_KEY_HERE",
        env: {
          GEMINI_API_KEY: "GEMINI_KEY_HERE"
        }
      },
      peekaboo: { enabled: true },
      sag: { enabled: false }
    },
    load: {
      watch: true,
      watchDebounceMs: 250,
      extraDirs: ["/path/to/shared/skills"]
    }
  }
}
```

```markdown
<!-- Example SKILL.md format -->
---
name: my-skill
description: Description of what this skill does
metadata: {"clawdbot":{"requires":{"bins":["uv"],"env":["API_KEY"]},"primaryEnv":"API_KEY"}}
---

Instructions for the agent on how to use this skill...
```

## Session Management

Configure session persistence, scoping, and lifecycle.

```bash
# Inspect sessions
clawdbot status
clawdbot sessions --json
clawdbot sessions --active 60    # Sessions active in last 60 minutes

# Query running gateway sessions
clawdbot gateway call sessions.list --params '{}'

# In-chat commands
# /new or /reset - Start fresh session
# /status - Check agent status and context usage
# /context list - View system prompt contents
# /compact - Summarize older context
# /stop - Abort current run
```

```json5
// ~/.clawdbot/clawdbot.json - Session configuration
{
  session: {
    scope: "per-sender",
    dmScope: "main",              // main | per-peer | per-channel-peer
    idleMinutes: 120,
    resetTriggers: ["/new", "/reset"],
    mainKey: "main",
    identityLinks: {
      alice: ["telegram:123456789", "discord:987654321012345678"]
    },
    sendPolicy: {
      rules: [
        { action: "deny", match: { channel: "discord", chatType: "group" } }
      ],
      default: "allow"
    }
  }
}
```

## Daemon Management

Control the background Clawdbot daemon service.

```bash
# Check daemon status
clawdbot daemon status

# Start/stop daemon
clawdbot daemon start
clawdbot daemon stop
clawdbot daemon restart

# View logs
clawdbot logs --follow

# Diagnostics
clawdbot doctor
```

## Gateway Configuration

Configure the Gateway server settings including auth, ports, and remote access.

```json5
// ~/.clawdbot/clawdbot.json - Gateway configuration
{
  gateway: {
    mode: "local",
    port: 18789,
    bind: "loopback",            // loopback | lan | tailnet | auto
    auth: {
      mode: "token",             // token | password
      token: "your-secret-token"
    },
    tailscale: {
      mode: "off",               // off | serve | funnel
      resetOnExit: false
    },
    remote: {
      sshTarget: "user@gateway-host",
      sshIdentity: "~/.ssh/id_rsa"
    }
  },
  agents: {
    defaults: {
      maxConcurrent: 3,
      mediaMaxMb: 5,
      sandbox: {
        mode: "non-main"
      },
      heartbeat: {
        every: "1h",
        model: "claude-3-opus"
      }
    }
  },
  routing: {
    agents: {
      main: {
        workspace: "~/clawd",
        sandbox: { mode: "off" }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Summary

Clawdbot serves as a unified AI assistant platform that bridges multiple messaging channels through a single Gateway server. Primary use cases include personal AI assistance via WhatsApp/Telegram DMs, team collaboration bots in Discord/Slack guilds, automated workflows via cron jobs and webhooks, and browser automation for web-based tasks. The pairing system ensures secure access control while the session management provides continuity across conversations.

Integration patterns typically involve running the Gateway as a background daemon, configuring channel credentials and allowlists, setting up skills for extended capabilities, and optionally connecting browser automation for web tasks. The CLI provides comprehensive management capabilities while the agent runtime handles LLM interactions, tool execution, and response delivery. Multi-agent setups allow routing different users or channels to specialized agents with their own workspaces and configurations.
